title: chelone glabra subsp. typica
intro: Chelone glabra subsp. typica, also known as the white turtlehead, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to eastern North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce fever and inflammation. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce cholesterol, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Chelone glabra subsp. typica can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions.
